channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Rubio says Iran ‘begging’ for a deal with US but not ready to make one
item.description: Secretary of state says US has 'a head for an eye' policy as strikes hit Iran for a 12th straight night.

item.title: Thai sailors sue owners of cargo ship hit in Hormuz
item.description: Thai sailors sue owners of cargo ship hit in Hormuz

item.title: Iran, Houthis strike tankers as US bombing continues: What’s the latest?
item.description: With the Houthi naval blockade, shipping disruptions now impact the Bab al-Mandeb strait in addition to Hormuz.

item.title: ‘Televising our revolution’: India’s Gen Z flips Modi’s social media game
item.description: For 12 years, Modi and his party have dominated Indian social media. Now, youth use the same platforms against him.

item.title: More than 2,300 Israelis storm Al-Aqsa Mosque in Jerusalem
item.description: National Security Minister Ben-Gvir joins settlers and far-right groups at holy site under heavy police protection.

item.title: ‘Back from the dead’: The continuing agony over Gaza’s 9,500 missing people
item.description: Palestinian's sudden return alive after family held his funeral highlights suffering of those still waiting for answers.

item.title: Indian activist Wangchuk sets conditions to end hunger strike
item.description: Indian activist Wangchuk sets conditions to end hunger strike

item.title: Photos: Gaza’s shelter crisis
item.description: Gaza faces a deepening housing crisis as more than 92 percent of homes have been damaged or destroyed by Israel.

item.title: Travellers panic as US attacks Iran-Iraq border crossing
item.description: Travellers panic as US attacks Iran-Iraq border crossing

item.title: India’s Modi promises fast-track courts for exam fraud fuelling protests
item.description: Cockroach Janta Party protesters are occupying streets in New Delhi, demanding Education Minister Pradhan resign.

item.title: Top US and Russian diplomats discuss Ukraine war in Manila
item.description: Russian Foreign Minister Lavrov 'reaffirmed ​readiness' for political ​and diplomatic settlement to Russia-Ukraine war.

item.title: Arsenal defender Saliba sidelined for ‘extended period’ due to back injury
item.description: Centre back William Saliba won't require surgery for injury sustained at World Cup, his club says.

item.title: Between hope and fear: Yemenis react to Houthi blockade on Saudi Arabia
item.description: Yemenis fear a maritime embargo on Saudi Arabia could reignite war, deepen shortages and worsen hunger.

item.title: Will the war on Iran affect ASEAN’s efforts to tackle regional issues?
item.description: Renewed fighting between the US and Iran has overshadowed the ASEAN gathering in Manila.

item.title: Thousands forced to evacuate as wildfires sweep across southern France
item.description: Thousands of people are being evacuated from areas of southern France as firefighters battle huge wildfires.

item.title: US attacks Iran for 12th night as the House approves defence spending
item.description: Strikes from the US on Iran are raining down for the 12th night in a row.

item.title: Saudi Arabia and US agree to landmark nuclear deal for civilian purposes
item.description: The US and Saudi Arabia have agreed to a civilian nuclear deal allowing Riyadh to build nuclear reactors.

item.title: Indian police accused of using pellet guns in crackdown on India’s protests
item.description: Videos emerge showing police using pellet guns on protesters India’s student protest movement.

item.title: Israel building earthen barrier in Gaza to entrench division of the enclave
item.description: The construction of the barrier has fuelled Palestinian fears that most of Gaza will never be relinquished by Israel.

item.title: Guyana ferry disaster: families demand answers as around 100 feared dead
item.description: Officials say 76 of the roughly 179 people believed to have been aboard survived, and 53 bodies have been found so far.

item.title: Jailed opposition leader Ghannouchi faints in Tunisian prison due to heat
item.description: The incident was witnessed by the daughter of Ahmed Nejib Chebbi, another prominent political prisoner.

item.title: Five soldiers killed in southern Thailand checkpoint attack
item.description: The assault in Narathiwat comes a day after a car bombing elsewhere in the province wounded a police officer.

item.title: Calls for Moroccan rapper’s release outside a Casablanca court
item.description: Protesters gathered outside a court in Casablanca, Morocco to demand the release of Moroccan rapper Mehdi El Youbi.

item.title: Armed attackers kill more than 20 in northwest Nigeria
item.description: The attacks reflect a surge in violence as armed gangs continue raiding villages in northwest and central Nigeria.

item.title: In Gaza, a long path to recovery for one woman who lost eye, and daughter
item.description: Psychological wounds compound Najwa's physical injuries after losing her daughter in an Israeli air strike.
